articles.headline: Wednesday briefing: From missing billions to nonexistent datacentres, inside Britain’s AI drive
articles.description: In today’s newsletter: Enormous investments in artificial intelligence promised to transform Labour’s growth problems. The reality is much murkierGood morning. For the last few years artificial intelligence has been heralded as the technology that will transform economies. Governments have promised billions in investment, datacentres and supercomputers, and a wave of new jobs as AI is “mainlined” into the economy. But what happens when the numbers behind those promises are examined more closely?That question sits at the centre of a series of recent investigations by the Guardian. The findings were remarkable – not least that the location for a much-trumpeted new supercomputer intended be up and running by year’s end to help fire up the British economy remains a scaffolding yard.Middle East crisis | Tehran residents say the Iranian capital has endured what they described as its worst night of aerial bombardment, as the US defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, followed Donald Trump’s suggestion on Monday the war could soon be over with a warning of more strikes to come.Environment | Climate breakdown is shrinking the amount of time that people can safely go about their lives, according to a study that shows a third of the world’s population now resides in areas where heat severely limits activity.Labour | Plans to curtail the number of jury trials in England and Wales have been described as “unpopular, untested and poorly evidenced” by thousands of lawyers who have written to the prime minister.UK economy | UK inflation could end the year higher than previously expected at 3% because of the US-Israel war in Iran, the government’s economics watchdog has warned.Travel | Tube drivers in London are to strike across 12 days in the spring in a row over a four-day working week, the RMT has announced. Continue reading...

hr.headline: Study Warns of 'AI Brain Fry' as Workers Oversee Too Many AI Agents

hr.why_care: This research highlights the importance of managing AI tool integration to prevent employee burnout and maintain productivity and retention within your workforce.
hr.what_to_do: Evaluate how many AI tools your employees are currently managing and consider implementing guidelines for AI workload to prevent cognitive overload.

hr.headline: U.S. Military Uses AI for Targeting, Oversight Remains 'Underinvested'

hr.why_care: While military-focused, this news underscores the critical need for robust AI governance and ethical oversight, which is a growing concern for all organizations adopting AI.
hr.what_to_do: Initiate a discussion with your leadership and legal teams about establishing clear ethical guidelines and oversight mechanisms for any AI tools your organization uses or plans to implement.

science_research.headline: Turns out, World's launching a tool to verify humans behind AI shopping agents
science_research.what_changed: Basically, Sam Altman's startup, World, is expanding its human verification tools. They're doing this because AI agents are starting to handle online shopping, and World wants to make sure there's a real person behind those AI actions.
science_research.why_care: For you, this highlights the growing need to verify human involvement in AI systems. As AI agents become more common, ensuring accountability and preventing misuse, even in commerce, is a challenge we're all watching.
science_research.what_to_do: Worth knowing: This push for verifying humans in AI interactions, even in shopping, really spotlights the bigger picture of accountability as AI gets more integrated into our lives. It's a theme you'll likely see pop up in research contexts too.

For you, this signals a broader shift in global financial infrastructure that could eventually influence how research grants are managed, how international scientific collaborations are funded, or how secure data transactions are processed.

operations_supply.why_care: This expansion highlights the growing energy demands of AI infrastructure, which could impact your future site selection, utility planning, and supply chain for energy resources.
operations_supply.what_to_do: Review your current energy consumption and infrastructure plans, considering potential future increases driven by AI technologies in your operations.

software_engineering.why_care: For you, this means as AI agents get smarter and start handling more of our online shopping, there's a growing need for robust identity verification. You might be building these agents, integrating with them, or needing to secure transactions they make.
software_engineering.what_to_do: Keep an eye on this: As AI agents get more autonomous, the need for solid identity verification is going to explode. This could mean new opportunities for you in building secure authentication or trust systems.

legal.headline: So, Sam Altman's 'World' is trying to verify humans behind AI shoppers

legal.why_care: For you, this gets at a big question: who's accountable when an AI agent acts on behalf of a human? As more transactions happen through AI, verifying the human behind the bot becomes crucial for things like contract formation, liability, and preventing fraud. You'll want to know if the 'person' agreeing to terms is actually a person.
legal.what_to_do: Keep an eye on this: As AI agents get better at shopping for us, the legal questions around who's responsible, how we prove consent, and even what counts as a valid contract are going to get super interesting. Knowing how human verification tech fits into all that will be really important for your work.

real_estate_construction.why_care: When financial giants like Mastercard invest this heavily in stablecoins, it signals a potential shift towards more widely adopted digital payment methods. For your real estate or construction business, this could eventually mean faster, more transparent, and potentially lower-cost ways to handle large transactions, from property sales to supplier payments.

As more AI agents handle transactions, knowing there's a verified human involved could impact how you think about fraud prevention and identity assurance in these new commerce models.

sales.headline: So, Worldcoin is trying to verify humans behind AI shopping agents
sales.what_changed: Basically, Sam Altman's company, Worldcoin, is expanding its human verification tech to work with AI shopping agents. This means when an AI agent is doing your online shopping, Worldcoin wants to make sure there's a real person behind that agent, especially for things like returns or disputes.
sales.why_care: For you, this highlights a growing challenge: how do you know if you're interacting with a human or an AI when customers are using AI agents to buy stuff? It's all about trust and authenticity in a world where AI is doing more of the buying.
sales.what_to_do: Keep an eye on this: As more customers use AI agents for shopping, understanding how those agents are verified, or if they even need to be, will become a key part of the customer journey. It could impact how you build trust or handle post-sale issues.
